input.command:
cd /app
nohup qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -m 1024 \
  -cdrom /app/alpine.iso \
  -drive file=/app/alpine-disk.qcow2,format=qcow2,if=ide \
  -boot d \
  -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait \
  -display none \
  -vga none \
  > /tmp/qemu.log 2>&1 &
echo "pid=$!"
sleep 3
echo "--- log ---"; cat /tmp/qemu.log 2>/dev/null | head -40
echo "--- listening? ---"; (ss -ltn 2>/dev/null || netstat -ltn 2>/dev/null) | grep 6665

ps aux | grep -i qemu | grep -v grep | head; echo "--- log ---"; cat /tmp/qemu.log; echo "--- port check ---"; python3 -c "import socket;s=socket.socket();print('open' if s.connect_ex(('127.0.0.1',6665))==0 else 'closed')"

input.command: python3 /tmp/probe.py 8 | tail -8; echo "=== reconnect test ok ==="

cat > /tmp/probe2.py <<'EOF'
import socket,time
s=socket.socket(); s.connect(('127.0.0.1',6665)); s.settimeout(2)
time.sleep(0.5)
s.sendall(b'\r\n')
time.sleep(1.5)
data=b''
try:
    while True: data+=s.recv(4096)
except socket.timeout: pass
out=bytearray(); i=0
while i<len(data):
    if data[i]==255: i+=3; continue
    out.append(data[i]); i+=1
print(out.decode('latin1'))
EOF
python3 /tmp/probe2.py

content.text:
Ready. The Alpine ISO is booted and running in the background under QEMU, with its serial console (getty on `/dev/ttyS0`) exposed as a telnet server on `127.0.0.1:6665`.

What I did:
- Launched `qemu-system-x86_64 -m 1024 -cdrom /app/alpine.iso -drive .../alpine-disk.qcow2 -boot d -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait -display none` (TCG, since `/dev/kvm` isn't present).
- Waited for the full boot; verified over a real socket connection that a fresh telnet session shows:
  ```
  Welcome to Alpine Linux 3.19
  Kernel 6.6.4-1-lts on an x86_64 (/dev/ttyS0)
  localhost login:
  ```

Run `telnet 127.0.0.1 6665` and you'll get the `localhost login:` prompt to log in. The QEMU telnet server re-listens after each disconnect, so your connection will work cleanly.

result:
Ready. The Alpine ISO is booted and running in the background under QEMU, with its serial console (getty on `/dev/ttyS0`) exposed as a telnet server on `127.0.0.1:6665`.

What I did:
- Launched `qemu-system-x86_64 -m 1024 -cdrom /app/alpine.iso -drive .../alpine-disk.qcow2 -boot d -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait -display none` (TCG, since `/dev/kvm` isn't present).
- Waited for the full boot; verified over a real socket connection that a fresh telnet session shows:
  ```
  Welcome to Alpine Linux 3.19
  Kernel 6.6.4-1-lts on an x86_64 (/dev/ttyS0)
  localhost login:
  ```

Run `telnet 127.0.0.1 6665` and you'll get the `localhost login:` prompt to log in. The QEMU telnet server re-listens after each disconnect, so your connection will work cleanly.
